title: BEM-876 analyst review
objective:
Create file governance/audit/internal/results/BEM876_ANALYST_PROTOCOL_V14_REVIEW.md with content # BEM-876 ANALYST PROTOCOL V1.4 REVIEW
Status: ANALYST_REVIEW_READY
Findings:
- v1.3 added useful details but weakened continuity with v1.2 high-level architecture. v1.4 must merge both layers.
- Terminology must distinguish role-objects from internal components. Director and Worker are role-objects. Curator is an internal component available inside Director, Worker, and General Director objects.
- Scaling has three levels: more internal contours inside one Worker, more Workers under a Director, and more Directors under a General Director.
- The rule system is missing and must be added as a central learning mechanism: errors, context conditions, decisions, solution patterns, and anti-duplicate logic.
- Communication model should be a single selected model: managed shared channel with Curator policy, plus separate event log.
- Integration and audit should not duplicate functions. Use internal self-check inside development contours, then a dedicated testing contour for combined result.
- Use рабочая область as the single term: contour workspace, testing workspace, main workspace.
- Roadmap must be detailed enough for assignment to Workers with IDs, dependencies, owners, and acceptance criteria.
Open practice additions:
- Use supervisor/orchestrator pattern, but map it to Curator component inside role-objects.
- Use handoffs, stateful workflows, typed outputs, guardrails, tracing, and human review for high-risk actions.
- Use deterministic-first and do not force LLM calls where rules and constraints are enough.
Recommendation:
- Approve v1.4 structure with expanded terms, rules hierarchy, scalable task registry, architecture registry, and detailed roadmap.
No issue comments.
